2603.15542.title: InterveneBench: Benchmarking LLMs for Intervention Reasoning and Causal Study Design in Real Social Systems
2603.15542.paper_summary: Causal inference in social science relies on end-to-end, intervention-centered research-design reasoning grounded in real-world policy interventions, but current benchmarks fail to evaluate this capability of large language models (LLMs). We present InterveneBench, a benchmark designed to assess such reasoning in realistic social settings. Each instance in InterveneBench is derived from an empirical social science study and requires models to reason about policy interventions and identification assumptions without access to predefined causal graphs or structural equations. InterveneBench comprises 744 peer-reviewed studies across diverse policy domains. Experimental results show that state-of-the-art LLMs struggle under this setting. To address this limitation, we further propose a multi-agent framework, STRIDES. It achieves significant performance improvements over state-of-the-art reasoning models. Our code and data are available at https://github.com/Sii-yuning/STRIDES.
2603.15542.paper_summary_zh: 因果推論在社會科學中依賴於基於現實政策干預的端到端、以干預為中心的研究設計推理，但目前的基準無法評估大型語言模型（LLMs）在這方面的能力。<lb/>我們提出了InterveneBench，一個旨在評估此類推理在現實社會環境中的基準。<lb/>InterveneBench中的每個實例均源自實證社會科學研究，並要求模型在沒有預定義因果圖或結構方程的情況下，對政策干預和識別假設進行推理。<lb/>InterveneBench包含744項跨多樣政策領域的同行評審研究。<lb/>實驗結果顯示，最先進的LLMs在這種情境下表現不佳。<lb/>為了解決這一限制，我們進一步提出了一個多代理框架STRIDES。<lb/>它在最先進的推理模型上實現了顯著的性能提升。<lb/>我們的代碼和數據可在https://github.com/Sii-yuning/STRIDES上獲得。
2603.15542.author: Shaojie Shi et.al.
2603.15542.authors: Shaojie Shi, Zhengyu Shi, Lingran Zheng, Xinyu Su, Anna Xie, Bohao Lv, Rui Xu, Zijian Chen, Zhichao Chen, Guolei Liu, Naifu Zhang, Mingjian Dong, Zhuo Quan, Bohao Chen, Teqi Hao, Yuan Qi, Yinghui Xu, Libo Wu
